meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no coding experience (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no coding experience. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Master SaaS Development Without Coding Skills
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between vision and execution. Use AI-driven Git workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your SaaS without writing code.
content.answer_block: You don't need a CS degree to build a SaaS. By leveraging AI-integrated Git workflows, you can translate business logic directly into production-ready code. Using tools like Antigravity to scaffold architecture and Claude Code to manage versioning, you can iterate, deploy, and scale your MVP at record speed.
content.intro:
The blank editor window is the graveyard of a thousand SaaS dreams. You have the market insight, the revenue model, and the burning desire to disrupt, but you hit the wall of 'no coding experience.' For years, this was a terminal bottleneck that forced founders to mortgage their equity to expensive dev shops.

But the paradigm has shifted. We are entering the era of the Software Architect-Founder, where your language is natural, not binary. By treating Git as your source of truth and AI as your relentless engineering team, you can bypass traditional roadblocks and ship products that actually scale. Let’s turn your vision into a repository.

steps.title: Architecting the MVP Schema
steps.description: Define the data structure and core functionality before writing a single line of logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Architect a scalable SaaS database schema for a subscription-based task manager using PostgreSQL. Provide the SQL schema and a JSON structure for the API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a new project directory and initialize a basic README.md with the architectural requirements defined in the schema.'

steps.title: Initializing the Git Repository
steps.description: Set up your version control environment to track changes and maintain a history of your AI-generated iterations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a .gitignore file optimized for a production-grade Node.js and TypeScript web application.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && claude run --prompt 'Create .gitignore and commit the initial structure to a new git branch named feature/mvp-setup'

steps.title: Generating Core Business Logic
steps.description: Use AI to write the functional code that powers your core value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for a Stripe-integrated checkout flow. Include error handling and validation logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the Stripe logic provided into the /api/checkout route and verify the code structure.'

steps.title: Reviewing and Committing Changes
steps.description: Audit the AI's output to ensure it aligns with your business goals before pushing to your repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Code Reviewer. Review the current directory for security vulnerabilities and code quality issues related to the newly implemented checkout flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Review the code, fix the identified issues, and create a commit with a descriptive message.'

steps.title: Deployment and Automation
steps.description: Connect your repository to a CI/CD pipeline to automate your deployment process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a GitHub Actions workflow file that automatically runs tests and deploys the code to Vercel upon merging to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Save the deployment workflow to .github/workflows/deploy.yml and commit the change.'

pro_tips: Always use atomic commits: Commit after every single AI-generated feature to make rollbacks trivial if a build breaks.
Define a 'System Prompt' for your AI agent that includes your preferred coding style, naming conventions, and security standards.
Treat your Git repository like a product log; write your commit messages for your future self, documenting the 'why' behind the logic, not just the 'what'.
Use AI to write your unit tests before the feature code; it forces the AI to understand the business requirements more deeply.
Never blindly accept AI code. Always use a secondary 'Reviewer' prompt to challenge the AI's own output for edge cases or security flaws.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, focus on collecting user feedback and using Claude Code to implement iterative updates based on real-world usage patterns.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting technical barriers dictate your business potential. By mastering the Git-AI workflow, you gain the autonomy to build, pivot, and scale at the speed of thought. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
